summary.intent: Address a third-pass Codex automated code review of PR #675 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing four concrete findings in RadioGroup and CheckboxGroup components, then get Codex approval.
summary.outcome: All four Codex findings were fixed (hidden input id, re-register move-to-end order, controlled max clamping on SetValue/SetProps/init), tests written and passing, CI green, commit pushed as third-pass fixes, review threads replied to and resolved, and a new @codex review triggered.

repo: The repo uses a Codex automated reviewer that signals status via GitHub reactions (eyes=reviewing, +1=approved) and inline review threads; polling both reactions and GraphQL reviewThreads is required to detect verdict.
Snapshot tests use `cargo insta`; after logic fixes that alter HTML output (e.g. adding `id` to hidden inputs), snapshots must be accepted with `cargo insta accept` before strict mode passes.
The gate sequence is: fmt (nightly) → clippy → spec conformance → spec validate → insta strict → targeted llvm-cov coverage.
CheckboxGroup and RadioGroup use a `Bindable` abstraction with controlled/uncontrolled slots; controlled max clamping must happen at three points: SetValue, SetProps sync, and init.
The project has a `snapshot-reviewed` label that must be re-applied after any commit that changes snapshot files.
Skills/agent behaviors are stored under `.agents/skills/` as SKILL.md files.

code.finding: CheckboxGroup `init` did not clamp the `Bindable`'s controlled slot to `max_checked`; fix required applying the clamp in the init path, not just in prop-sync transitions.
RadioGroup hidden input was missing an `id` attribute; two snapshots (normal and disabled states) needed updating after the fix.

workflow: Polling a GitHub PR for automated reviewer status requires checking both reactions (for eyes/+1) and GraphQL reviewThreads (for new unresolved threads); using only one signal leads to missed verdicts.
A malformed GraphQL query in a polling loop silently returns no results; always validate query syntax before putting it in a watch loop.
Running focused `cargo test -p <crate> --lib <module>` first to confirm new regression tests fail as expected before applying fixes is an effective TDD workflow.
Parallel `sleep 60; <command>` calls can be used to stagger polling checks without a shell loop process.

friction: The initial long-running `while true; sleep 60` polling loop had a malformed GraphQL query string that silently returned wrong thread counts, requiring a switch to direct polling.
A stray background polling process needed manual verification via `ps` after a user missclick interrupted the session.
Nightly `cargo fmt` produced wrapping-only changes in the new patches that required a format-fix-recheck cycle before proceeding.
Codex review latency required multiple polling rounds (10+ minutes) to get from `eyes` to new thread verdicts.
open_items: CI still had Coverage, Feature Flags — Dioxus, and Release Verification jobs pending at the time of the third-pass commit; their final status was not confirmed in this transcript.
The fourth Codex review pass had been triggered but no verdict was received by the end of the transcript — approval or further findings remain outstanding.
The `ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs` diff was inspected but not explicitly confirmed clean after the fmt run.
